stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump hints at talks; US blockade in Hormuz enters 2nd day
stories.summary: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as blockade of Iranian ports enters a second day.

stories.headline: HSBC Private Bank Cuts India Stocks, Adds Gold on Iran War Risks
stories.summary: HSBC Private Bank and Premier Wealth has downgraded emerging Asian equities and slashed exposure to India, while raising allocations to gold, cash and hedge funds on Iran war risks and oil price shocks.

stories.headline: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
stories.summary: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.headline: JD Vance Heckled In Antiwar Protest at Turning Point USA Event
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance appeared to express sympathy with critics of the war with Iran: “I recognize that young voters do not love the policy we have in the Middle East, OK. I understand.”

stories.headline: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
stories.summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

stories.headline: Gallego Dismissed Rumors of a ‘Flirty’ Swalwell, Highlighting a Culture of Silence
stories.summary: The admission by Senator Ruben Gallego that he had heard, but disbelieved, rumors about Eric Swalwell and women showed the attitude on Capitol Hill toward men accused of behaving badly.

stories.headline: China's more-than-decade-late sanctions ineffective as leverage on Japan
Pimco Buys All $400 Million of Bonds Sold by Blue Owl BDC
stories.summary: Pacific Investment Management Co. bought all $400 million of bonds issued Monday by a Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

US, Iran Seek More Ceasefire Talks Amid Blockade
stories.summary: The US and Iran are looking to arrange a second round of peace talks in the coming days, while a standoff in the Strait of Hormuz worsens a global energy crisis and complicates diplomatic prospects. Bloomberg's Jeff Mason and Weilun Soon break down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.summary: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.headline: U.S. blockade has turned back six merchant ships leaving Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: More than a dozen American warships positioned in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea are acting as a “net,” officials said. None of the encounters has required escalation.

stories.headline: How US blockade on Iran will worsen energy crisis
stories.summary: With the US military blockade of Iranian ports now in force, global energy markets are already reacting.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
stories.summary: The meeting marks the first direct talks between the two sides, though US officials say more time is needed.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.headline: How the US can blockade the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US forces will be ready to descend from helicopters on to oil tankers

stories.headline: Israeli attack kills several people in Gaza refugee camp
stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza recorded victims of an Israeli strike on the Shati refugee camp being rushed to a medical facility.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy pitches new joint security system to European allies
stories.summary: Ukraine should be an integral part of Europe’s security, or some on the continent would be part of the "Russian world," Zelenskyy said as he announced discussions over a new joint defence system with EU and non-EU countries.

stories.headline: China Evergrande’s billionaire boss pleads guilty to fraud

stories.headline: Fifth woman accuses former US lawmaker Eric Swalwell of sexual misconduct
stories.summary: The Democratic representative has resigned his seat in Congress over multiple sexual misconduct allegations.

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?
stories.summary: BBC News journalist Simon Atkinson explains how the couple's tour will be different compared to their last visit.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: OpenAI releases new cyber security model to limited group of customers
stories.summary: Launch of GPT-5.4-Cyber follows concern about ability of Anthropic’s Mythos to find software bugs

stories.headline: Daiichi Life to Tighten Private Credit Manager Selection on Risk
stories.summary: Daiichi Life Group Inc. is tightening the process for selecting managers of private credit investments to decrease risk after several high-profile defaults overseas.

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Europe’s Economy Has Slipped Below ECB Baseline
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said higher energy costs have pushed the euro zone away from the European Central Bank’s base-case outlook, though not enough yet to warrant leaning toward raising interest rates.

stories.headline: Traders Target Bond Market Rally on Iran War ‘Tone Change’
stories.summary: Optimism about a possible peace deal in the Middle East and lower oil prices has bond traders positioning for gains in Treasuries that could send 10-year yields sliding toward 4%.

stories.headline: Dollar and VIX Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
stories.summary: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
stories.summary: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

stories.headline: Resignations of Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales Set Up Special Election Fights in Texas and California
stories.summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California called a special election to replace Mr. Swalwell, while Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas had yet to announce one for Mr. Gonzales’s successor.

stories.headline: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
stories.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
stories.summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

stories.headline: Survivors ask why busy market bombed in Nigerian anti-terror campaign
stories.summary: Military has described devastating attack that killed up to 200 people, many of them civilians, as a ‘precision airstrike’Survivors and observers have questioned the Nigerian military’s rationale for a devastating airstrike on a busy market that killed as many as 200 people, many of them civilians.The hit on Jilli market on the border of the north-eastern Borno and Yobe states on Saturday is the latest in a string of attacks by the country’s air

stories.headline: Health of boy thrown from Tate Modern takes ‘sad step backwards’, family say
stories.summary: French child, six at time of 2019 attack, suffers setback in recovery after January operationThe family of a boy thrown from the 10th-storey balcony of the Tate Modern seven years ago said it feels as though his recovery has taken a “sad step backwards” after surgery.The unnamed French child was six when he was seriously hurt in an attack by Jonty Bravery at the London attraction in August 2019. Continue reading...

stories.headline: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: BlackRock profits jump as it draws in $130bn
stories.summary: Earnings for first quarter underline asset manager’s push into investment products that generate higher fees

stories.headline: Latham partners pocket record $8.7mn average pay
stories.summary: US law firm’s revenues surpass $8bn for first time on the back of megamerger mandates

stories.headline: Asian Stocks Rise, Oil Drops on US-Iran Peace Push: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks tracked Wall Street higher and oil declined as optimism over renewed US-Iran talks buoyed sentiment.

stories.headline: Yancoal Buys Australia Mine Stake for Up to $2.4 Billion
stories.summary: Chinese-controlled Yancoal Australia Ltd. agreed to acquire an 80% stake in the Kestrel coking coal mine in Queensland from a venture between EMR Capital Advisors Pty and Adaro Capital Ltd. for as much as $2.4 billion.

stories.headline: Commerzbank CEO at Odds With Unicredit Over Valuation
stories.summary: Commerzbank CEO Bettina Orlopp says that she has had some talks with UniCredit SpA about a deal after the Italian bank announced a takeover bid for the German lender. She speaks with Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: America’s corporate boards are under siege
stories.summary: Activist shareholders and regulatory changes are undermining their control

stories.headline: Virginia Governor Ends Tax Breaks for Confederate Groups
stories.summary: The new law signed by Gov. Abigail Spanberger is the culmination of a long, Democrat-led push to distance Virginia from its Confederate past.

stories.headline: Spain’s Bangladeshi community cheers migrant amnesty programme
stories.summary: Crowds from the Bangladeshi community in Barcelona celebrated the approval of a mass amnesty programme.

stories.headline: Rape retrial of disgraced Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein begins in New York court
stories.summary: The judge declared a mistrial last June after the jury foreperson refused to return to deliberate the case amid a jury-room feud.

stories.headline: EU auditors question new fund backed by EU carbon border tax for polluting industries
stories.summary: EU auditors argue the Temporary Decarbonisation Fund, designed to channel revenues from the EU's carbon border tax to help heavy industries cope with the energy transition, may do little to drive new green investment.

stories.headline: Oldest gorilla in captivity celebrates 69th birthday in Berlin Zoo
stories.summary: Arriving in Berlin in 1959, the western lowland gorilla has far outlived the typical 35-40 year wild lifespan through specialised care.

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Closes Near Record on Iran Peace Push: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks surged on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 Index closing within sight of a fresh record as optimism on the possibility of further peace talks between the US and Iran pushed down oil prices.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: How war has made a 33-year-old the Czech Republic’s richest man

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies
